description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging midsection, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three prominent metal hoops. The wood texture is weathered, showing natural grain and some discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top and bottom are open, revealing the interior, which appears hollow and unfinished. The metal bands are slightly tarnished, adding to the rustic appearance. The object is robust and utilitarian, suitable for storage or transport of goods. Its design is typical of barrels found in urban alleys, storage yards, or near workshops.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, open at both ends and showing a rustic, aged appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood grain, and three metal hoops encircling the barrel.
description_view_1: Side-by-side comparison of the textured wooden barrel and a simplified, untextured version, highlighting the metal hoops and bulging shape.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the untextured side, emphasizing the geometric structure and band placement.
description_view_3: Side-by-side view with the wooden texture on the right and the untextured model on the left, showing the full curvature and banding.
